title: Lastrea deltoidea
intro: Lastrea deltoidea, also known as Delta-leaf fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea deltoidea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lastrea deltoidea include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Lastrea deltoidea is classified within the family Athyriaceae and the order Polypodiales. This species belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical regions. The classification of Lastrea deltoidea also places it within the class Polypodiopsida and the division Pteridophyta. Further, it is considered a species of vascular plant, characterized by its leafy fronds and reproductive structures. The specific classification of Lastrea deltoidea reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history among other fern species.
